title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA reduced diffusion denoising steps from fifty to as few as one by combining dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation. The approach stacks multiple optimization layers that can be applied independently or together to achieve 10x to 200x speedup for real-time video generation on Blackwell hardware.
writeup: Ziv Ilan of NVIDIA presents techniques for accelerating video diffusion, demonstrating real-time generation on the Blackwell B200 by cutting the default 50-step denoising process. The methods combine quantization, latent caching, and step distillation, where a student model is trained to match a teacher's output in as few as one to eight steps. These optimizations are composable and can stack to deliver 10 to 200x speedup, which matters for making diffusion inference cheap enough to run interactively.
